title: Demo Flow
business_process: Show, in two sessions, that the org's own research memory — including its failures — reaches the next researcher before they repeat the work.

acceptance_criteria.text: A single command loads the seed graph from scratch, and the abandoned finding F-093 is present with its reason intact.
Session 1 runs live against the MCP server and the new finding lands in the graph with provenance.
In Session 2 — a new session with no shared context — the agent surfaces both the prior finding and the abandoned attempt with its reason, and never uses the word 'novel'.
From a cold start, the Cairn tools are callable in Claude Desktop.
The wiki page renders from the post-demo graph and cites both demo findings.
The cross-indication graph visual renders from the post-demo graph with no hand-drawn nodes.
A sub-five-minute mp4 or mov recording of the complete two-session flow exists before demo day.

open_questions: The demo depends on the live TCGA result from survival-analysis. If that slips, Session 1 has no real number to write — see review-queue.md row 1.
